Executive Summary:
• 	Now that you have a solid (conceptual) understanding of the Product Launch
Formula, it’s time to go through some exercise to help prepare you for your launch.
• 	Remember, there is no straight line to success. It’s about taking baby steps and the
correcting as you go.
• 	You don’t have to be perfect on these exercise, you just have to get started. Success is
about getting started.
• 	You’re not going to do a million dollar launch the first time out.
• 	Go through the next three sections and do the exercises.
• 	Remember - it’s about incremental change.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

TRANSCRIPT
MODULE ONE – Your Launch
3
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. 	I 	www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
TRANSCRIPT:
Now you’ve got a solid core understanding of Product Launch Formula.
It’s pretty conceptual at this point, I understand that. We’re about to start
nailing it down and getting really detailed. Now it’s time to get to work on your
launch. This next set of videos will be walking you through some exercises to help
prepare you for your launch.
The thing to remember is there is no straight line to success. It’s all
about taking those baby steps and then course correction. The first time you go
through these exercises that doesn’t mean you’re going to just completely rock it. You
can visit them and revisit them and revisit them as often as you want.
When I think of course correction, I often think of golf.
I actually played a bunch of golf when I was a young teen; I worked at a golf
course for a summer and played golf for an entire summer. I’ve hardly played it since
then. But it really is an improbable sport.
The thing is you’re sitting there with this little golf ball and you have to hit
that ball, on a Par 5, 500 yards into a hole this big. 500 yards is almost a third of a
mile away that you have to hit this tiny ball into this tiny little hole. It’s just
absolutely ludicrous to think about that. And you’re hitting this with a stick. It’s not
easy, it’s just an improbable sport.
The idea of hitting that ball and having it go straight to the hole and taking
you four or five shots and have it go straight every time – first you hit it 250 yards,
the next time you hit it 200 yards, and you hit it straight to the hole – that never
happens.
Even the greatest golfer in the world on their best hole of their lives, they
don’t hit every shot directly at the hole. What they do is they hit it towards the hole.
The greatest golfers might hit directly very close to perfect, but it’s still not perfect.
Amateurs, they just sort of hit it in that general direction. Then the next time they hit
it, they’re hitting it in the general direction. Each time they’re getting closer to the
hole and their corrections are generally less and less, until they’re standing over a
putt. Maybe it’s taken them 10 or 12 shots to get there, but they’re only a foot away
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 3 of 5 --

TRANSCRIPT
MODULE ONE – Your Launch
4
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. 	I 	www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
and now it’s a lot easier to knock it into the hole.
You gradually course correct as you get closer. It’s like here’s where you’re
starting and here’s your destination. We all want to do this, but in real life it never
happens that way. In real life, you go like this and you realize that you’re not
completely on course, so you go back here, maybe next time you’re over here, and
next time you’re over here, but you’re gradually getting closer and closer.
That’s the funny thing. I’ve been out with a lot of beginning golfers and no
matter how improbable it is, they generally, even when they’re first starting out, will
get the ball in the hole within 10 or 12 shots. Now, 10 or 12 shots might not be great
for an experienced golfer, but it is sort of improbable that you have this little ball and
this little hole a long ways away and in just 10 or 12 shots by course correcting you
gradually get closer and closer.
I’m just trying to encourage you.
You don’t have to be perfect on these exercises, you just have to
get started. Success is not a straight line. It’s just about getting started.
You’re not going to do a million dollar launch the first time out, it’s just not
going to happen. Unless you have a serious business. You have a few people who
their first launch they’ve done a million, but they had a serious business with a big
list. If you’re just starting out or you just have an average size business, you’re not
going to do a million dollar launch the first time.
Look at my path. My first launch was $1,650. John Gallagher’s path, his first
launch was $20,000. Susan’s first launch $27,000. Those, I think, are outstanding,
nothing wrong with those. Occasionally you have exceptions and people do even
better. We had Jane Savoy do a six figure launch in the massage market with this
little tiny list, but that’s a rare exception. Usually you’re starting out much smaller.
Think about my first launch of $1,650 and how it got better and better and
eventually I did that million-dollar hour.
In this next section we’re going to go through three exercises.
1. We’re going to identify your launch goals.
2. We’re going to do an asset identification for you.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

3. Then we’re going to go through your avatar.
This is about baby steps, it’s about incremental change, it’s about getting it
20% right and then working from there and improving it from there.
Please, be sure to do the work. We’re going to be leveraging that work.
It’s the building blocks, we’re going to be leveraging that work throughout all the
other modules. This material will directly apply to all of your launches.
Your Seed Launches, Quick Launches, Internal Launches, JV Launches, even
your re- launches, ecommerce launches, every one of your launches. This stuff is
really very important.
